studies.title: A randomized, controlled clinical trial to evaluate the efficacy of the digital Cognitive Behavioral Therapy "somnio junior" to reduce symptoms of insomnia  in adolescens suffering from insomnia after three months of application

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates 'somnio junior', a variation of the 'somnio' digital therapeutic product, developed by mementor DE GmbH. The trial registration and full text confirm the focus on this specific product for treating insomnia in adolescents.

_classification.reason: The study is described as a randomized, controlled clinical trial, which involves random allocation of participants to an intervention group and a control group, fulfilling the criteria for an RCT.

studies.title: Effects of digital cognitive-behavioral psychotherapy for insomnia (dCBT-I) KVT-I on depression

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the digital therapeutic product 'somnio' by mementor DE GmbH, as mentioned in the trial description and scientific summary. The intervention is clearly identified as 'somnio', a digital application for cognitive-behavioral therapy for insomnia.

_classification.reason: The study uses an individually randomized, parallel waitlist-controlled design where participants are randomly assigned to either receive the digital intervention immediately or after a delay, which is characteristic of a randomized controlled trial.

studies.title: Investigating the effects of digital cognitive behavioral psychotherapy of insomnia (dCBT-I) on sleep in insomnia

_verification.reason: The study specifically investigates the effects of 'somnio', a digital cognitive-behavioral therapy for insomnia developed by mementor DE GmbH, as mentioned in the scientific summary and observational groups.

_classification.reason: The study uses an individually randomized, parallel group design to compare the effects of digital cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia against an active control group. Randomization and controlled conditions are key features of an RCT.

_verification.reason: The study explicitly mentions the use of the 'Somnio' app for delivering cognitive-behavioral therapy for insomnia as part of the intervention. This directly links the study to the specific digital therapeutic product 'somnio'.

_classification.reason: The study is described as a 'randomised and controlled TraumaSleep Study', indicating that it involves random allocation of participants to intervention and control groups, which is characteristic of an RCT.

studies.title: Efficacy of digital cognitive-behavioural psychotherapy for the treatment of insomnia (dCBT-I) in comorbid chronic pain

_verification.reason: The study specifically mentions the 'somnio' therapy program, which consists of 11 modules and is used for digital cognitive behavioural therapy for insomnia. The product name 'somnio' is explicitly mentioned in the intervention description.

_classification.reason: The study involves a comparison between a group receiving digital cognitive-behavioural therapy and a control group that does not receive the digital therapy, indicating random allocation to intervention and control groups, characteristic of an RCT.

studies.title: Effects of a digital cognitive-behavior therapy for insomnia (CBT-I)

_verification.reason: The study specifically mentions 'somnio' as the digital therapeutic product being tested for insomnia, as indicated by the URL 'www.somn.io' and the description of the intervention group using 'somnio'.

_classification.reason: The study involves an intervention group receiving digital cognitive-behavior therapy for insomnia and a control group on a waiting list, indicating random allocation to treatment versus control under controlled conditions.
